name: Amsterdam
localTips: Skip paper tickets entirely — tap in and out on GVB trams and metros with any contactless bank card, and remember to check out or you'll be charged the maximum fare.
Never walk in the reddish bike lanes — cyclists have right of way and genuinely will not stop for you.
Anne Frank House tickets are released online exactly six weeks in advance and sell out within hours — there is no ticket office at the door.
Avoid the restaurants along Damrak and Leidseplein and eat where locals do in De Pijp or the Jordaan for half the price.
The free GVB ferries behind Centraal Station run all night to Amsterdam Noord — they're the cheapest canal cruise in town.

activities.title: Wander the Jordaan's canals
activities.description: Drift along Egelantiersgracht and Bloemgracht past leaning gabled houses, houseboats and tiny galleries in Amsterdam's prettiest quarter.

activities.title: De 9 Straatjes boutique browsing
activities.description: Nine tiny streets crossing the canal belt, packed with vintage shops, concept stores and cheese ateliers — the shops make it a fine rainy-day wander too.

activities.title: Vondelpark people-watching
activities.description: Amsterdam's great green lung — join joggers, picnickers and the open-air theatre crowd, then find the free summer concerts at the bandstand.

activities.title: Begijnhof hidden courtyard
activities.description: Step through an unmarked door off Spui into a silent 14th-century courtyard with Amsterdam's oldest wooden house and a clandestine chapel.

activities.title: Bloemenmarkt floating flower market
activities.description: The world's only floating flower market — stalls on houseboats along the Singel selling tulips, bulbs and enough Delft-blue kitsch to sink a barge.

activities.title: Free ferry to NDSM wharf
activities.description: Ride the free GVB ferry from behind Centraal Station across the IJ to NDSM, a shipyard turned street-art playground with Europe's biggest graffiti murals.

activities.title: Stadsarchief treasure vault
activities.description: The City Archives' art-deco Bazel building shows Rembrandt's burial record and Anne Frank documents in a free basement vault of black marble.

activities.title: Civic Guard Gallery (free)
activities.description: The Amsterdam Museum's glass-roofed Schuttersgalerij is a free public street hung with giant 17th-century group portraits — a mini Rijksmuseum in passing.

activities.title: OBA library rooftop terrace
activities.description: The vast Openbare Bibliotheek at Oosterdok has seven floors of design-magazine calm and a top-floor café terrace with a free skyline panorama.

activities.title: Albert Cuyp street market
activities.description: Amsterdam's biggest street market slices through De Pijp — try a fresh warm stroopwafel from the original stand while browsing 260 stalls under their awnings.

activities.title: Westerpark & Westergas terrain
activities.description: A landscaped park flowing into the Westergas complex — a Victorian gasworks reborn as breweries, bakeries and arthouse cinemas.

activities.title: Magere Brug by night
activities.description: The Skinny Bridge over the Amstel glows with 1,200 lightbulbs after dark — the classic Amsterdam night photo, with the river houseboats twinkling beyond.

activities.title: Old town canals after dark
activities.description: The medieval heart around Oudezijds Voorburgwal is at its most atmospheric at night — neon reflections, the Oude Kerk floodlit, and centuries of history per block.

activities.title: Rijksmuseum sculpture gardens
activities.description: The formal gardens wrapped around the Rijksmuseum are free to enter, with rotating outdoor sculpture exhibitions and the iconic museum facade behind.

activities.title: Amsterdam Light Festival
activities.description: Every winter, international artists hang glowing installations over the canals — walk the route through the centre for free after dark.

activities.title: Brown café hour at 't Smalle
activities.description: A candle-lit 1786 jenever distillery turned brown café on Egelantiersgracht — dark wood, sand on the floor and locals who've had the same stool for decades.
activities.insiderNote: The canal-side terrace is on a floating pontoon — arrive before 17:00 to claim it, or squeeze inside by the stove when it rains.

activities.title: Jenever tasting at Wynand Fockink
activities.description: A 1679 tasting house hidden in an alley behind Dam Square, pouring dozens of jenevers and liqueurs from ancient barrels — no stools, no music, all ritual.
activities.insiderNote: The glass is filled to the absolute brim: local custom is to bend down, hands behind your back, and take the first sip without lifting it.

activities.title: De Foodhallen food court
activities.description: A 1902 tram depot in Oud-West turned indoor food hall — bitterballen from De Ballenbar, dim sum, G&T bar and a young local crowd every night of the week.
activities.insiderNote: Weeknights before 18:30 you'll actually find a table; Friday and Saturday nights are standing-room only by 20:00.

activities.title: Apple pie at Winkel 43
activities.description: The towering cinnamon-heavy appeltaart at this Noordermarkt corner café is a city institution — warm, with a mountain of whipped cream.
activities.insiderNote: Come on Saturday morning and combine it with the Noordermarkt farmers' market outside — but order the pie first, the queue moves fast.

activities.title: Hunt the Jordaan's hidden hofjes
activities.description: The Jordaan hides dozens of almshouse courtyards behind plain doors — find the Karthuizerhof and the Claes Claesz Hofje for silent gardens metres from busy streets.
activities.insiderNote: Most hofjes are open weekdays roughly 10:00–17:00; push the door gently and keep voices low — people still live around every courtyard.

activities.title: Free lunch concert at the Concertgebouw
activities.description: Most Wednesdays at 12:30 the world-famous Concertgebouw opens its doors for a free 30-minute concert — often a public rehearsal of that week's programme.
activities.insiderNote: Doors open at 12:15 and seats go fast — queue by noon at the side entrance; no tickets needed, just walk in.

activities.title: Pllek beach bar at NDSM
activities.description: A restaurant-bar built from shipping containers with an artificial beach on the IJ — Noord locals come for sunset drinks and the view back across the water.
activities.insiderNote: Take the free NDSM ferry over; in winter the container interior with its wood stove is arguably even cosier than the summer beach.

activities.title: SkyLounge rooftop bar
activities.description: The 11th-floor bar of the DoubleTree by Centraal has the best open-to-the-public panorama in the city — canals, harbour and old town in one sweep.
activities.insiderNote: No reservation needed if you arrive before 17:00; the indoor lounge keeps the full view when the weather turns.

activities.title: Surinamese roti in De Pijp
activities.description: Amsterdam's colonial history left it with brilliant Surinamese food — Warung Spang Makandra's roti and pom sandwiches are the local go-to.
activities.insiderNote: Order the roti kip with extra pepper sauce and eat it the local way, with your hands — takeaway is cheaper if the few tables are full.

activities.title: Rijksmuseum entry
activities.description: Rembrandt's Night Watch, Vermeer's Milkmaid and eight centuries of Dutch art in the country's grandest museum — book a morning slot to beat the tour groups.

activities.title: Van Gogh Museum timed entry
activities.description: The world's largest Van Gogh collection — Sunflowers, The Bedroom and the letters that tell his story. Timed tickets only, and they sell out days ahead.
activities.bookingQuery: Van Gogh Museum timed entry ticket Amsterdam

activities.title: Anne Frank House
activities.description: Climb behind the bookcase into the Secret Annex where Anne Frank wrote her diary — quietly devastating and unmissable.

activities.title: Evening canal cruise
activities.description: Glide under 1,200 lit bridges through the UNESCO canal ring in a glass-roofed boat — the city's gables are at their best reflected in dark water.

activities.title: Heineken Experience
activities.description: The original 1867 brewery turned interactive tour — brewing halls, stables, a 4D ride and two draught beers at the rooftop bar to finish.

activities.title: Rembrandt House Museum
activities.description: The house where Rembrandt lived and went bankrupt, rebuilt room by room from his own inventory — daily etching demonstrations in his actual studio.

activities.title: Moco Museum (Banksy & modern art)
activities.description: A townhouse museum on Museumplein filled with Banksy, Warhol and immersive digital rooms — small, punchy and very photogenic.

activities.title: A'DAM Lookout & swing
activities.description: A 360° observation deck atop the A'DAM tower across the IJ, with Europe's highest swing tipping you out over the edge, 100 metres up.

activities.title: Zaanse Schans windmills half-day
activities.description: Working 18th-century windmills, clog carvers and cheese makers along the river Zaan, 40 minutes from the city — the Holland postcard made real.
activities.bookingQuery: Zaanse Schans windmills half day tour from Amsterdam

activities.title: Jordaan food & history tour
activities.description: Graze through the Jordaan with a local guide — herring from a proper haringhandel, aged Gouda, stroopwafels and jenever, with canal history between bites.

activities.title: Keukenhof tulip gardens day trip
activities.description: Seven million bulbs in bloom at the world's most famous spring garden, 35 minutes from Amsterdam — open only mid-March to mid-May.
activities.bookingQuery: Keukenhof gardens ticket with transfer from Amsterdam
